query: longest river in the world Amazon Nile length measurements scientific consensus
answer: The Amazon River is often considered the world's longest river at 4,225 miles (6,800 km), according to Brazilian scientists, while the Nile River is traditionally held at 4,132 miles (6,650 km). The debate continues, but most scientists still regard the Nile as the longest.

results.title: What's the World's Longest River? | Live Science
results.snippet: Flowing northward through the tropical climate of eastern Africa and into the Mediterranean Sea, the Nile river is the longest river in the world at 4,135 miles (6,650 kilometers), according to the U.S. National Park Service. The Amazon River is 3,980 miles (6,400 kilometers) long, according to the U.S. National Park Service. It is, however, the world's largest river by volume and contains 20 percent of the Earth's fresh water, according to the National Science Foundation. In 2007, Brazilian scientists declared that they had re-measured the Amazon during a 14-day expedition, and calculated that the river's length to be 4,225 miles (6,800 kilometers), therefore making it longer than the Nile, according to their results. Overall, the current consensus within the geographic community is that the Nile remains the holder of the longest river title, with the Amazon coming in a close second place, according to the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO).

results.title: What is the longest river in the world? It's surprisingly debatable
results.snippet: Most would say the Nile but some scientists say that the Amazon is the longest river in the world. It is from there that the Nile is measured as the world’s longest river. ## Isn’t the Amazon the longest river in the world? And to make the dispute even more interesting, if the Mantaro River really is the source, that would add 47 to 57 miles (75 to 92 kilometers) to the length of the Amazon. In 2007, Brazilian researchers announced they had identified a new source and mouth for the Amazon, which made the river 4,225 miles (6,800 kilometers) long, toppling the Nile as the longest river. By this measure, the Amazon is clearly the largest river in the world with a drainage area of 6.3 million square kilometers, while the Nile makes it only to the fifth spot, trailing behind the Congo, the Mississippi, and the Ob. results.title: Amazon Longer Than Nile River, Scientists Say | National Geographic
results.snippet: The Amazon is considered the world's largest river by volume, but scientists have believed it is slightly shorter than Africa's Nile.
